# Framein

> Framein keeps one local work frame across Claude, Codex, Gemini, and AI coding agents.

Framein helps developers start with one agent, challenge the work with another, switch lead model when needed, and close the work with validation. It keeps a shared local task contract, decision trail, risk state, validation results, and model-switch capsule underneath the agent workflow they already use.

## Canonical Pages

- English overview: https://www.framein.dev/
- Korean overview: https://www.framein.dev/ko
- Source repository: https://github.com/framein-dev/framein
- Code signing policy: https://github.com/framein-dev/framein/blob/main/docs/CODE_SIGNING.md
- Company: https://frameout.co.kr

## Naming

- Prose product name: Framein
- Logo, CLI command, npm package, binary, and file/id form: framein
- Aliases: fr, frame
- Agent command namespaces: /fr:* for Claude and Gemini, $fr-* for Codex

## Core Commands

- framein init: create local state and project managed blocks
- framein start "<goal>": define the task contract
- framein challenge "<proposal>" --run: ask a bounded independent reviewer
- framein capsule [agent]: prepare a model switch from local facts
- framein verify: run configured build and test validation
- framein risk: inspect sensitive diff areas
- framein ship: check whether the work is ready to ship

## Trust Boundary

Framein is local-first. It does not collect provider credentials, pool subscriptions, relay MCP tools, or screen-scrape terminal I/O. Provider authentication stays with the official CLI for each provider.

## Code Signing Policy

Windows release signing is being prepared through SignPath Foundation OSS signing. The public policy covers signing roles, privacy, uninstall, and verification: https://github.com/framein-dev/framein/blob/main/docs/CODE_SIGNING.md

## Current Status

Framein is pre-release. Public executable distribution is not yet marked ready until CI artifacts, signing, notarization, and smoke tests are complete.
